feed.title: The Git Times
feed.subtitle: AI-generated newspaper for builders — daily coverage of trending GitHub projects

entry.title: Zed’s AI-Powered Multiplayer Editing Reaches New Maturity
entry.summary: “Civilization advances by extending the number of important operations which we can perform without thinking about them.” — Alfred North Whitehead

entry.title: Scrcpy v4.0 Shifts to SDL3, Refines Android Screen Mirroring
entry.summary: “Tools for conviviality are those which give each person who uses them the greatest opportunity to enrich the environment with the fruits of their vision.” — Ivan Illich

entry.title: Prometheus v3.12.0 Hardens Security and Expands Cloud Discovery
entry.summary: “Everywhere we remain unfree and chained to technology, whether we passionately affirm or deny it.” — Martin Heidegger

entry.title: Chinese Fiction Pipeline Automates Novel Writing with LLMs
entry.summary: “The question concerning technology is never merely technical.” — Martin Heidegger

entry.title: New Texture Ripper Lets Developers Extract Game Assets from Screenshots
entry.summary: “Consider what the world would lose if each mind were to do its own indexing.” — Vannevar Bush

entry.title: OpenFugu Unlocks Sakana's Closed LLM Orchestrator for Public Use
entry.summary: “New technology is not additive; it is ecological. A new medium does not add something; it changes everything.” — Neil Postman

entry.title: Bohemia Interactive Open-Sources Classic Arma Engine Codebase
entry.summary: “The information you have is not the information you want. The information you want is not the information you need.” — Neil Postman

entry.title: Cloudflare Open-Sources AI-Powered Security Audit Skill for Developers
entry.summary: “Technology is the knack of so arranging the world that we don't have to experience it.” — Max Frisch

entry.title: OfficeCLI Lets AI Agents Read, Edit, and Automate Office Files Without Installing Office
entry.summary: “We are what we behold. We shape our tools and then our tools shape us.” — John Culkin

entry.title: A Browser-Based Bird Flock Simulator Soothes Digital Fatigue
entry.summary: “We drive into the future using only our rearview mirror.” — Marshall McLuhan

entry.title: Claude Plugin Reshapes Academic Research With Disciplined Human Oversight
entry.summary: “The best way to predict the future is to invent it.” — Alan Kay

entry.title: Graphify Turns Codebases Into Queryable Knowledge Graphs for AI
entry.summary: “We are called to be architects of the future, not its victims.” — Buckminster Fuller

entry.title: CodeGraph Supercharges Claude Code with Local Knowledge Graphs
entry.summary: “You never change things by fighting the existing reality. To change something, build a new model that makes the existing model obsolete.” — Buckminster Fuller

entry.title: External ImGui Overlay Enhances GTA V Without Code Injection
entry.summary: “Progress imposes not only new possibilities for the future but new restrictions.” — Norbert Wiener

entry.title: Multica v0.3.1 Elevates AI Agents as True Development Teammates
entry.summary: “The best material model of a cat is another, or preferably the same, cat.” — Norbert Wiener

entry.title: Awesome Go List Evolves to Meet AI and Cloud Demands
entry.summary: “The major problems of the world are the result of the difference between how nature works and the way people think.” — Gregory Bateson

entry.title: Symphony Frees Teams From Supervising AI Coding Agents
entry.summary: “The best way to predict the future is to invent it.” — Alan Kay

entry.title: Veteran Engineer Releases Skills to Elevate AI Beyond Vibe Coding
entry.summary: “We are called to be architects of the future, not its victims.” — Buckminster Fuller

entry.title: Stash Adds Persistent Memory to AI Agents
entry.summary: “You never change things by fighting the existing reality. To change something, build a new model that makes the existing model obsolete.” — Buckminster Fuller

entry.title: Proxy Unlocks Free Claude Code for Terminal and VSCode Users
entry.summary: “Progress imposes not only new possibilities for the future but new restrictions.” — Norbert Wiener
